function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

Law Firm Wiki compiler. Compile PI practice knowledge from ARCHIVED case activity logs.

1. Read /opt/data/FirmVault/wiki/AGENTS.md for the full schema
2. Read ALL existing articles in /opt/data/FirmVault/wiki/concepts/ and /opt/data/FirmVault/wiki/connections/
3. For each case in /tmp/batch_arch_2_cases.txt (231 cases), read activity logs in cases/<slug>/Activity Log/. For cases with >50 log files, sample first 10 + last 10 chronologically.
4. UPDATE existing articles: increment evidence_count, upgrade confidence (5+=medium, 10+=high), add new evidence bullets
5. CREATE new articles only for genuinely new patterns not covered
6. ANONYMIZE all PII
7. Write to /opt/data/FirmVault/wiki/concepts/ and wiki/connections/
8. Append summary to /opt/data/FirmVault/wiki/log.md
9. Do NOT rewrite wiki/index.md

These are ARCHIVED cases with COMPLETE lifecycles. Focus on: full case lifecycle patterns, intake-to-close timelines, settlement outcomes, decline reasons, negotiation progressions, lien resolution, disbursement workflows, case type distribution.
I'll start by reading the schema, case list, and existing wiki articles in parallel.

# Law Firm Wiki — Compiler Schema

> This file is the specification for the LLM that compiles activity logs
> into structured knowledge articles. Read it in full before any compile
> or query operation.

## What You Are

You are a knowledge compiler for a personal injury law firm. Your job is
to read raw case activity logs and extract reusable institutional knowledge
— the process, pitfalls, patterns, and best practices that make a PI
practice work.

You are NOT creating a case database. You are building a **wiki** — a
structured collection of concept articles about HOW PI cases work, derived
from evidence in real cases.

## The Three Operations

### 1. COMPILE (ingest raw → update wiki)

Read activity logs and case files. For each meaningful pattern you identify:

1. Check `index.md` — does a concept article already exist?
2. If yes → UPDATE it: add new evidence, adjust confidence, update Related
3. If no → CREATE it: full article per the Article Schema below
4. If two concepts interact → create/update a Connection article
5. Update `index.md` with any new or modified articles
6. Append a summary to `log.md`

**What to extract:**
- Process patterns (what happens and in what order)
- Decision points (why certain choices were made)
- Problems and resolutions (what went wrong, how it was fixed)
- Timeline patterns (how long things actually take)
- Communication patterns (what works with adjusters, clients, providers)
- Edge cases (unusual situations and how they were handled)
- Anti-patterns (things that caused problems)

**What to IGNORE:**
- Individual client PII (names, SSN, DOB, addresses, phone numbers)
- Specific dollar amounts tied to identifiable cases
- Attorney-client privileged communications
- Raw email addresses and phone numbers
- Replace specifics with anonymized references: "Case A", "Case B", or
  descriptive patterns: "in cases involving [treatment gap > 30 days]"

**Confidence scoring:**
- high: Pattern observed in 5+ cases with consistent outcomes
- medium: Pattern observed in 2-4 cases
- low: Single case observation, or conflicting evidence

### 2. QUERY (ask questions → synthesize answers)

1. Read `index.md` to identify 3-10 relevant concept articles
2. Read those articles in full
3. Synthesize an answer with [[wikilink]] citations
4. If the answer reveals a gap → note it in the answer
5. Optionally file the Q&A back into `wiki/qa/`

### 3. LINT (health check)

Run these checks and write results to `wiki/reports/`:
- **Broken wikilinks**: Links that point to non-existent articles
- **Orphan articles**: Concepts with zero inbound links
- **Phase coverage**: Which PHASE_DAG phases have sparse knowledge
- **Stale articles**: Source data changed since last compile
- **Contradictions**: Articles that contain conflicting guidance

## Article Schema

### Concept Article (wiki/concepts/*.md)

```yaml
---
title: Human-Readable Title
slug: kebab-case-slug
phase: [phase_0_onboarding]    # which PHASE_DAG phases this applies to
category: one-of-below
tags: [tag1, tag2, tag3]
confidence: high               # high/medium/low
evidence_count: 5              # number of cases with supporting evidence
created: YYYY-MM-DD
updated: YYYY-MM-DD
related:                       # other concept slugs
  - related-concept-one
  - related-concept-two
---

# Title

> One-paragraph summary (2-3 sentences max)

## Pattern

Detailed explanation of the pattern, process, or concept.

## Evidence

Anonymized observations from source cases:
- "In N cases where [condition], [outcome]"
- "When [action was taken], [result]"

## Agent Guidance

Actionable instructions for paralegal agents:
1. Step one
2. Step two
3. Step three

## Pitfalls

- Common mistake one
- Common mistake two

## Related

- [[related-concept-one]] — how it connects
- [[related-concept-two]] — how it connects
```

### Categories

- `process`: Standard workflows and procedures
- `adjuster-tactics`: Insurance company behavior patterns
- `edge-cases`: Unusual situations requiring special handling
- `phase-knowledge`: Phase-specific best practices
- `communication`: Client/adjuster/provider communication patterns
- `financial`: Billing, liens, settlements, funding
- `medical`: Treatment patterns, records, chronologies
- `legal`: Litigation, discovery, depositions, trial
- `tools`: Software, systems, and process tools

### Connection Article (wiki/connections/*.md)

Same schema but `category: connection` and the body explains how
two or more concepts interact. Example:

```
treatment-gaps-affect-demand-value.md
→ Links: treatment-gap-defense + demand-timing-strategy
→ Insight: Cases with documented gap explanations recovered 80% of
  expected value vs 50% for unexplained gaps
```

### Q&A Article (wiki/qa/*.md)

```yaml
---
title: Question text
slug: question-slug
date: YYYY-MM-DD
sources:                       # concept articles consulted
  - concept-slug-one
  - concept-slug-two
---

# Question

[Full question text]

## Answer

[Synthesized answer with [[wikilink]] citations]

## Gaps Identified

[Any knowledge gaps the question revealed]
```

## Index Schema (wiki/index.md)

The master catalog. This is the LLM's entry point for all queries.

```markdown
# Law Firm Wiki — Index

Last compiled: YYYY-MM-DD | Articles: N | Connections: N

## By Phase

### Phase 0: Onboarding
| Article | Category | Confidence | Evidence |
|---------|----------|------------|----------|
| [[concepts/onboarding-pitfalls]] | phase-knowledge | high | 15 |

### Phase 1: File Setup
...

## By Category

### Adjuster Tactics
| Article | Phase | Confidence |
|---------|-------|------------|
| [[concepts/treatment-gap-defense]] | 2,3 | high |
...

## Recent Updates

| Date | Article | Action |
|------|---------|--------|
| 2026-04-12 | treatment-gap-defense | created |
```

## Activity Log Format Reference

Activity logs live at `cases/<slug>/Activity Log/*.md` and have this structure:

```yaml
---
schema_version: 2
case_id: uuid
date: "YYYY-MM-DD"
time: "HH:MM:SS"         # optional
category: legal|correspondence|imported|financial|medical|task
subcategory: email|filevine_negotiation|outbound_call|...
---

# Category — Date

**Case:** [[cases/slug/slug|Client Name]]
**Source:** description

[Free-form content: emails, notes, call summaries, task descriptions]
```

### Subcategory Distribution (21K+ logs)
- email: 3,078
- filevine_negotiation: 1,620
- outbound_call: 890
- missed_call: 780
- filevine_treatment: 717
- filevine_demandinprogress: 645
- demand: 534
- filevine_filesetup: 369
- phase_change: 254
- filevine_uim: 179
- inbound_call: 137
- esignature: 78
- call_summary: 10

## PHASE_DAG Reference

The case lifecycle has 8 phases:
- Phase 0: Onboarding (contract, HIPAA, intake)
- Phase 1: File Setup (insurance claims, provider setup)
- Phase 2: Treatment (monitoring, records collection)
- Phase 3: Demand (drafting, review, sending)
- Phase 4: Negotiation (offers, counters, strategy)
- Phase 5: Settlement (agreement, disbursement)
- Phase 6: Lien Resolution (PIP waterfall, lien negotiation)
- Phase 7: Litigation (complaint, discovery, trial)
- Phase 8: Closed

## Rules

1. NEVER include identifiable client information in wiki articles
2. Anonymize all case references (use "Case A", "Case B", or descriptive patterns)
3. Every article must have at least one [[wikilink]] to a related concept
4. Update index.md whenever you create or modify an article
5. Append to log.md whenever you compile
6. Confidence scores must reflect actual evidence count
7. When in doubt about a pattern's validity, use confidence: low
8. Prefer specific actionable guidance over vague advice
9. Group knowledge by PHASE_DAG phase when possible
10. Flag contradictions explicitly — don't silently resolve them

# FirmVault — Agent Orientation

> **Read this in full at the start of every session.** It names the thesis, the non-negotiable rules, and where to find the authoritative docs. Short deliberately — use the imports below for detail.

## What this repo is

FirmVault is the **development environment for an AI paralegal backend** intended to eventually be sold to other personal-injury law firms. This specific repo is Whaley Law Firm's own instance; the architecture we prove here becomes the product.

Two parallel product tracks exist:

1. **This repo** — the GitHub-native paralegal backend: markdown vault, `PHASE_DAG.yaml`, `SKILL.md` library, runtime (materializer + worker), intake pipeline.
2. **Separate Huly fork** (not in this repo) — the lawyer-facing UI. This repo's "UI" is for operators, not lawyers.

## The thesis

**"The firm as a code repo."** Law firm work is repeatable tasks + templates + rules, which is exactly what code is. Put the firm's work into primitives agents already excel at (issues, branches, PRs, diffs) and you get a much better AI experience than any traditional PMS bolt-on.

## Core architectural principles

1. **The vault is a shadow, not the source of truth.** Real files (signed PDFs, audio, emails, faxes) live on the firm's own storage (their server, Dropbox, Box, Drive). The markdown in `cases/<slug>/` is a PHI-masked projection of those files.
2. **Intake is a pipeline, not an agent**: document arrives → real file placed on user's storage → converted to markdown → PHI stripped → opens a PR against this repo. The PR is the first human-in-the-loop gate.
3. **Git is the deliberate interface.** Every agent action is a commit or PR. Free audit trail, free approval gates, trivial rollback. Agents *never* touch real files — only their masked shadows.
4. **Markdown, not a graph DB.** We tried FalkorDB and abandoned it. LLM agents are empirically more reliable on plain text + grep than on Cypher queries. This is a deliberate choice, not laziness.
5. **PHI never lives in the vault.** Placeholders like `{{client_ssn}}` and `{{dob}}` reference secrets held in the user's own infrastructure. At render time, deterministic code (not the model) substitutes real values. The model never sees raw PII.
6. **The vault is the only state store for this layer.** No external database. No per-case JSON files. No FalkorDB. No `${ROSCOE_ROOT}`. See `@skills.tools.workflows/DATA_CONTRACT.md` for the authoritative mapping.

## Do NOT do these things

These are actively deprecated. If you see them in old code or docs, treat them as bugs.

- **Do not reference FalkorDB, Cypher, `graph_client`, `graph_manager`, or `run_cypher_query`.** All removed. Everything reads the vault.
- **Do not reference per-case JSON files** (`overview.json`, `contacts.json`, `insurance.json`, `liens.json`, `expenses.json`, `medical_providers.json`, `case_state.json`). Deleted.
- **Do not reference `${ROSCOE_ROOT}`** or any absolute-path scaffolding. Everything is repo-relative.
- **Do not edit content between `<!-- roscoe-medical-start -->` / `<!-- roscoe-medical-end -->` or `<!-- roscoe-insurance-start -->` / `<!-- roscoe-insurance-end -->` markers.** Those blocks are owned by the import scripts and will be overwritten on next sync.
- **Do not invent new vault paths.** If the thing you want to write doesn't have a home in `DATA_CONTRACT.md`, update the contract first (same commit), then write. Drift between contract and reality is the bug.
- **Do not create top-level dashboards, reports, or state files outside the vault layout**. Operator views read the vault via GitHub API or git; they don't create parallel state.

## Where the authoritative docs live

Import these at launch — they are the contract, not me paraphrasing:

@DESIGN.md

@skills.tools.workflows/DATA_CONTRACT.md

@MEMORY.md

Also non-imported but worth knowing:

- `skills.tools.workflows/workflows/PHASE_DAG.yaml` — machine-readable phase definitions, landmarks, and predicate vocabulary. The materializer reads this.
- `skills.tools.workflows/runtime/README.md` — how the materializer and worker agents drive the task queue.
- `skills.tools.workflows/runtime/task_schema.md` — the contract for task issues (labels, frontmatter, lifecycle).
- `Templates/INDEX.md` — the firm template library (73 slugified templates grouped by category).
- `skills.tools.workflows/Skills/*/SKILL.md` — 42 modernized skills in Anthropic Agent Skills format. Exemplars: `lien-management`, `deposition-prep`, `deposition-strategy`, `pip-application`.

## Slug rules (critical for wikilinks)

From `DATA_CONTRACT.md` §4:

- Lowercase
- Apostrophes and curly quotes **stripped** (no replacement char): `K'daya` → `kdaya`
- `&` → `and`
- All other non-alphanumeric runs collapsed to a single hyphen
- Leading/trailing hyphens stripped

Mismatched slugs break wikilinks across the vault. Slug before you write.

## Runtime model (what's being built)

The system is being turned from passive documentation into an active work-driving loop:

```
Vault (cases/*) ← materializer (cron) → GitHub Issues ← worker (event) → Vault
```

- **Materializer** walks open cases, reads each case's `landmarks:` frontmatter + `PHASE_DAG.yaml`, opens a GitHub Issue for every unsatisfied landmark whose task template preconditions are met. Already prototyped as a prompt in `runtime/materializer_prompt.md`.
- **Worker** is triggered by the `status:ready` label on an issue, claims it, loads the named skill, runs it, commits vault changes that reference the task_id, and flips the issue status. Prototyped in `runtime/worker_prompt.md`.
- **Source of truth** for whether a landmark is satisfied is `case.frontmatter.landmarks.<id>` — the materializer checks this first and only falls back to evaluating a predicate if the key is missing.

## Working branch

All in-progress work lives on `claude/import-cases-from-drive-xiQgL`. Do not push directly to `main` without an explicit request.

## When in doubt

- Read `DATA_CONTRACT.md` before writing to the vault.
- Read `PHASE_DAG.yaml` before touching workflow/landmark logic.
- Read `MEMORY.md` to find out what's in flight and what's pending.
- Check the last 3–5 commits on the working branch to see the most recent decisions.

# Adjuster File Closure Threats

> Insurance adjusters — both PIP and BI — will threaten to close their claim file when the firm fails to respond to repeated outreach attempts. This is distinct from the adjuster-unreturned-calls pattern (where the adjuster is trying to settle); here the adjuster is explicitly warning that inaction will result in lost benefits or a closed claim. File closure threats are most dangerous on PIP claims where benefits may be forfeited.

In long-running cases, the firm may interact with 3-5 different adjusters on a single claim. Understanding this pattern helps manage expectations and maintain negotiation momentum.

# Attorney Withdrawal from Active Litigation

> When a case is in active litigation and the attorney-client relationship breaks down, the attorney cannot simply close the file — they must file a Motion to Withdraw with the court. This is a more complex closing process than pre-litigation decline because the firm is counsel of record. Client non-cooperation (especially failure to appear for depositions) is the primary trigger observed. Defense counsel may simultaneously file motions for sanctions and dismissal.

When a case manager leaves or cases are reassigned, the incoming staff member must pick up an unfamiliar file, establish rapport with the client, and continue without losing momentum. > In some PI cases, a state child support enforcement agency asserts a lien against the client's settlement proceeds. Unlike medical or insurance liens, child support liens arise from pre-existing family court obligations and can represent extremely large amounts relative to the case value. These liens are legally distinct from subrogation liens and may not be negotiable in the same way.

> PI clients exhibit a predictable pattern of escalating frustration when they feel the firm is not communicating with them. This pattern typically begins with polite check-ins, escalates to demands for immediate response, and can culminate in threats to change attorneys. # Client-Initiated Termination of Representation

> In some cases, the client fires the firm rather than the firm declining the case. This can happen at any phase — from early file setup through active litigation. The termination is typically communicated via email or text, and the firm must immediately change the case status to Closing, send the standard closing letter, and notify all carriers and lien holders. This pattern is distinct from firm-initiated decline (coverage gaps, client MIA) because the client is an active participant who has decided to leave.

# COOTS Letter and UIM Claim Trigger

> When a BI carrier tenders policy limits and the client has UIM coverage, a "COOTS letter" (Coots v. Allstate, a Kentucky case) must be sent to the UIM carrier before accepting the BI settlement. The COOTS letter notifies the UIM carrier of the BI limits offer and gives them an opportunity to consent to the settlement or preserve their subrogation rights. This is a mandatory procedural step in Kentucky that triggers the parallel UIM claim track.

Coverage denials can add years to case timelines and require attorney involvement from day one.

# Kentucky Assigned Claims (KAC) Process

> When the at-fault driver in a Kentucky MVA has no valid insurance coverage (due to coverage denial, policy lapse, or being uninsured), the plaintiff can file a claim through the Kentucky Assigned Claims Plan (KAC). KAC assigns the claim to a carrier (e.g., Nationwide) who acts as the PIP carrier for medical bill payments. The KAC process differs from standard PIP in its documentation requirements, bill submission rules, and the potential for disputes about which bills are covered.

# Robinson v. Bates Medical Specials Reduction

> In Kentucky PI cases, adjusters commonly cite Robinson v. Bates to reduce the valuation of medical specials. Under this legal principle, the insurer pays only what the "collateral source" (health insurance, Medicaid, PIP) actually paid for treatment, rather than the full billed amount. This reduces the firm's leverage when negotiating, as the adjuster values the case based on allowed/paid amounts rather than provider billing.

> The statute of limitations (SOL) is the absolute deadline for filing a lawsuit. In Kentucky PI cases, this is typically 2 years from the date of accident. Approaching the SOL without resolution requires escalation to the attorney for a decision: settle at current offer, file suit, or withdraw. SOL monitoring is automated in Filevine with countdown alerts.

> When police reports or liability investigation reveals the firm's own client was at fault, the case must be declined regardless of the client's injuries or treatment. This is a distinct decline trigger from coverage gaps (no BI/UM) — here the coverage may exist, but there is no viable defendant to pursue. The discovery of client fault may occur weeks or months into file setup, after significant work has already been invested.

# Closing Phase Triggers Inappropriate Review Request

> When a case moves to "Closing" status in Filevine, an automated task fires: "Do you think client will give 5 star review? If so reach out to client and send link." This task fires indiscriminately — including on cases where the client was at fault, the client fired the firm, the case was declined due to no coverage, or the client was unreachable for months. Requesting a review from these clients would be inappropriate and potentially damaging to the firm's reputation.

# Release Signing Delays Block PIP Carrier Reimbursement

> After a BI claim settles, the at-fault carrier often owes the client's PIP carrier a reimbursement (PIP subrogation). However, the BI carrier cannot process this reimbursement until the signed release is returned. When the firm delays returning the signed release — whether due to client non-response, internal processing delays, or release term disputes — the PIP carrier is blocked from receiving its subrogation payment. This creates a cascade: the BI adjuster calls repeatedly requesting the release, the PIP adjuster may escalate, and the client may face continued billing pressure from providers who expected PIP to have been reimbursed.

from datetime import datetime

VAULT = "/opt/data/FirmVault"
CASES_FILE = "/tmp/batch_arch_2_cases.txt"
COUNTS_FILE = "/tmp/case_log_counts.txt"

# Read case list
with open(CASES_FILE) as f:
    cases = [line.strip() for line in f if line.strip()]

# Read case log counts
case_counts = {}
with open(COUNTS_FILE) as f:
    for line in f:
        parts = line.strip().split('|')
        if len(parts) == 2 and parts[1] != 'NODIR':
            case_counts[parts[0]] = int(parts[1])

# Pattern tracking
patterns = {
    'case_types': Counter(),  # mva, wc, premise, etc.
    'subcategories': Counter(),
    'phase_changes': [],  # (case, from_phase, to_phase, date)
    'decline_reasons': [],  # (case, reason)

    'pip_mentions': [],  # (case, context)

}

# Case type extraction from slug
def get_case_type(slug):
    slug_lower = slug.lower()
    if '-wc-' in slug_lower or slug_lower.endswith('-wc'):
        return 'workers_comp'
    elif '-premise-' in slug_lower or '-premise' in slug_lower:
        return 'premises_liability'
    elif '-mva-' in slug_lower:
        return 'mva'
    elif '-db-' in slug_lower:
        return 'dog_bite'
    elif '-sandf-' in slug_lower:
        return 'slip_and_fall'
    elif '-pi-' in slug_lower:
        return 'general_pi'
    elif '-a-b-' in slug_lower:
        return 'assault_battery'
    else:
        return 'unknown'

# PII anonymization
def anonymize(text):
    """Strip PII from text for pattern extraction."""
    # Remove names from Case lines
    text = re.sub(r'\*\*Case:\*\*\s*\[\[.*?\]\]', '**Case:** [REDACTED]', text)
    # Remove email addresses
    text = re.sub(r'[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.\w+', '[EMAIL]', text)
    # Remove phone numbers
    text = re.sub(r'\b\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b', '[PHONE]', text)
    # Remove SSN
    text = re.sub(r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b', '[SSN]', text)
    # Remove DOB patterns
    text = re.sub(r'\b(?:DOB|Date of Birth|dob)\s*[:=]\s*\d{1,2}[/-]\d{1,2}[/-]\d{2,4}', 'DOB: [REDACTED]', text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    # Remove addresses (simplified)
    text = re.sub(r'\d+\s+[A-Z][a-z]+\s+(?:St|Ave|Rd|Dr|Ln|Blvd|Way|Ct|Pl)\b\.?,?\s*(?:[A-Z][a-z]+\s*,?\s*[A-Z]{2}\s*\d{5})?', '[ADDRESS]', text)
    return text

def extract_date_from_frontmatter(content):
    """Extract date from YAML frontmatter."""
    match = re.search(r'date:\s*["\']?(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})', content)
    if match:
        return match.group(1)
    return None

def extract_subcategory(content):
    """Extract subcategory from frontmatter."""
    match = re.search(r'subcategory:\s*(\S+)', content)
    if match:
        return match.group(1)
    return None

def extract_category(content):
    """Extract category from frontmatter."""
    match = re.search(r'^category:\s*(\S+)', content, re.MULTILINE)
    if match:
        return match.group(1)
    return None

# Pattern detection functions
def detect_patterns(slug, content, case_type):
    """Detect all relevant patterns from a log entry."""
    content_lower = content.lower()
    anon = anonymize(content)
    date = extract_date_from_frontmatter(content)
    subcat = extract_subcategory(content)
    
    if subcat:
        patterns['subcategories'][subcat] += 1
    
    # Phase changes
    if subcat == 'phase_change' or 'phase change' in content_lower:
        phase_match = re.search(r'(?:changed?|moved?|transition)\s+(?:to|from)\s+["\']?([^"\']+)', content_lower)
        if phase_match:
            patterns['phase_changes'].append((slug, phase_match.group(1), date))
        # Look for specific phase names
        for phase in ['file setup', 'treatment', 'demand in progress', 'negotiation', 
                       'settlement', 'closing', 'lien', 'litigation', 'lead', 'potential new client']:
            if phase in content_lower:
                patterns['phase_changes'].append((slug, phase, date))
    
    # Decline/closing patterns
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['decline', 'closing letter', 'disengage', 'case closed', 'no coverage', 'no bi', 'no um']):
        reason = 'unknown'
        if 'no coverage' in content_lower or 'no bi' in content_lower or 'no um' in content_lower:
            reason = 'no_coverage'
        elif 'client at fault' in content_lower or 'client was at fault' in content_lower:
            reason = 'client_at_fault'
        elif 'unreachable' in content_lower or 'cannot reach' in content_lower or 'unable to contact' in content_lower:
            reason = 'unreachable_client'
        elif 'fired' in content_lower or 'terminate' in content_lower or 'new attorney' in content_lower:
            reason = 'client_terminated'
        elif 'statute' in content_lower or 'sol' in content_lower:
            reason = 'sol_expiring'
        elif 'no treatment' in content_lower or 'not treating' in content_lower:
            reason = 'no_treatment'
        elif 'decline' in content_lower:
            reason = 'general_decline'
        patterns['decline_reasons'].append((slug, reason, date))
    
    # Settlement mentions
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['settlement', 'settled', 'settle for', 'settlement check', 'release signed']):
        patterns['settlement_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Negotiation patterns
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['offer', 'counter', 'counteroffer', 'counter-offer', 'policy limit', 'policy limits', 'final offer']):
        if subcat in ('filevine_negotiation', None) or 'offer' in content_lower:
            patterns['negotiation_rounds'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Lien mentions
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['lien', 'subrogation', 'conduent', 'medicaid lien', 'medicare', 'zipliens', 'child support']):
        lien_type = 'unknown'
        if 'medicaid' in content_lower or 'conduent' in content_lower:
            lien_type = 'medicaid'
        elif 'medicare' in content_lower:
            lien_type = 'medicare'
        elif 'va ' in content_lower or 'veterans' in content_lower:
            lien_type = 'va'
        elif 'child support' in content_lower:
            lien_type = 'child_support'
        elif 'health insurance' in content_lower or 'anthem' in content_lower or 'humana' in content_lower or 'united' in content_lower or 'cigna' in content_lower:
            lien_type = 'health_insurance'
        elif 'zipliens' in content_lower:
            lien_type = 'zipliens_service'
        else:
            lien_type = 'general'
        patterns['lien_mentions'].append((slug, lien_type, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Disbursement
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['disbursement', 'disburse', 'check mailed', 'check cut', 'funds distributed']):
        patterns['disbursement_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Demand
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['demand sent', 'demand letter', 'demand package', 'demand in progress', 'demand drafted']):
        patterns['demand_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Treatment patterns
    if subcat in ('filevine_treatment',) or any(w in content_lower for w in ['treatment complete', 'discharged', 'mmi', 'maximum medical improvement', 'done treating']):
        patterns['treatment_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # PIP
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['pip', 'personal injury protection', 'pip ledger', 'pip exhausted', 'pip claim']):
        patterns['pip_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # UIM
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['uim', 'underinsured', 'coots', 'consent to settle']):
        patterns['uim_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Litigation
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['complaint filed', 'lawsuit', 'litigation', 'deposition', 'discovery', 'mediation', 'motion to']):
        patterns['litigation_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Client communication patterns
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['client called', 'client angry', 'client frustrated', 'client wants update', 'client threatening']):
        patterns['client_communication'].append((slug, 'frustration', anon[:200], date))
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['voicemail full', 'unable to reach', 'no answer', 'disconnected number']):
        patterns['unreachable_client'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Adjuster patterns
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['new adjuster', 'reassigned', 'transferred to']):
        patterns['adjuster_patterns'].append((slug, 'reassignment', anon[:200], date))
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['closing file', 'close the file', 'file will be closed']):
        patterns['adjuster_patterns'].append((slug, 'file_closure_threat', anon[:200], date))
    
    # Release delays
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['release', 'vinesign', 'sign the release', 'release received', 'release sent']):
        patterns['release_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Records collection
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['records request', 'medical records', 'records received', 'hipaa', 'records outstanding']):
        patterns['records_collection'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # WC specific
    if case_type == 'workers_comp' or any(w in content_lower for w in ['workers comp', 'ttd', 'form 110', 'brc', 'dwc']):
        patterns['wc_patterns'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Premise specific
    if case_type in ('premises_liability', 'slip_and_fall'):
        patterns['premise_patterns'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Collections anxiety
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['collections', 'collection agency', 'credit', 'bills going to']):
        patterns['collections_anxiety'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # SOL mentions
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['statute of limitation', 'sol ', 'sol:', 'two year', '2 year', '2-year']):
        patterns['sol_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # File transfer
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['prior counsel', 'prior attorney', 'file transfer', 'new counsel']):
        patterns['file_transfer'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Minor settlement
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['minor', 'guardian', 'blocked account', 'stable account', 'court approval']):
        patterns['minor_settlement'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Google review
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['google review', '5 star', 'five star', 'review request']):
        patterns['google_review'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Pre-settlement funding
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['funding', 'lawsuit loan', 'pre-settlement', 'presettlement']):
        patterns['funding_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Lost wages
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['lost wages', 'wage verification', 'salary verification', 'missed work']):
        patterns['lost_wages'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Companion cases
    if any(w in content_lower for w in ['companion', 'family member', 'same accident', 'passenger']):
        patterns['companion_cases'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # E-signature
    if subcat == 'esignature' or 'vinesign' in content_lower or 'e-sign' in content_lower:
        patterns['esignature_mentions'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))
    
    # Intake
    if subcat == 'filevine_filesetup' or any(w in content_lower for w in ['welcome call', 'retainer', 'intake', 'new client']):
        patterns['intake_patterns'].append((slug, anon[:200], date))

# Process all cases
processed = 0
total_logs_read = 0

for slug in cases:
    case_type = get_case_type(slug)
    patterns['case_types'][case_type] += 1
    
    log_dir = os.path.join(VAULT, 'cases', slug, 'Activity Log')
    if not os.path.isdir(log_dir):
        continue
    
    log_files = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(log_dir, '*.md')))
    count = len(log_files)
    
    # Sample if >50
    if count > 50:
        sampled = log_files[:10] + log_files[-10:]
    else:
        sampled = log_files
    
    # Track timeline
    dates = []
    
    for lf in sampled:
        try:
            with open(lf, 'r', encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore') as f:
                content = f.read()
            total_logs_read += 1
            date = extract_date_from_frontmatter(content)
            if date:
                dates.append(date)
            detect_patterns(slug, content, case_type)
        except Exception as e:
            pass
    
    if dates:
        dates.sort()
        patterns['timeline_data'].append((slug, case_type, dates[0], dates[-1], count))
    
    processed += 1

# Build summary statistics
summary = {
    'total_cases': len(cases),
    'processed': processed,
    'total_logs_read': total_logs_read,
    'case_types': dict(patterns['case_types']),
    'subcategory_distribution': dict(patterns['subcategories'].most_common(30)),
    'phase_changes_count': len(patterns['phase_changes']),
    'decline_count': len(patterns['decline_reasons']),
    'settlement_count': len(patterns['settlement_mentions']),
    'negotiation_count': len(patterns['negotiation_rounds']),
    'lien_count': len(patterns['lien_mentions']),
    'disbursement_count': len(patterns['disbursement_mentions']),
    'demand_count': len(patterns['demand_mentions']),
    'treatment_count': len(patterns['treatment_mentions']),
    'pip_count': len(patterns['pip_mentions']),
    'uim_count': len(patterns['uim_mentions']),
    'litigation_count': len(patterns['litigation_mentions']),
    'unreachable_count': len(patterns['unreachable_client']),
    'records_count': len(patterns['records_collection']),
    'wc_count': len(patterns['wc_patterns']),
    'premise_count': len(patterns['premise_patterns']),
    'collections_count': len(patterns['collections_anxiety']),
    'sol_count': len(patterns['sol_mentions']),
    'release_count': len(patterns['release_mentions']),
    'funding_count': len(patterns['funding_mentions']),
    'lost_wages_count': len(patterns['lost_wages']),
    'companion_count': len(patterns['companion_cases']),
    'minor_count': len(patterns['minor_settlement']),
    'google_review_count': len(patterns['google_review']),
    'file_transfer_count': len(patterns['file_transfer']),
    'intake_count': len(patterns['intake_patterns']),
}

# Decline reason breakdown
decline_reasons = Counter(r[1] for r in patterns['decline_reasons'])
summary['decline_reasons'] = dict(decline_reasons)

# Lien type breakdown
lien_types = Counter(r[1] for r in patterns['lien_mentions'])
summary['lien_types'] = dict(lien_types)

# Timeline analysis
timelines = patterns['timeline_data']
if timelines:
    durations = []
    for slug, ctype, first, last, count in timelines:
        try:
            d1 = datetime.strptime(first, '%Y-%m-%d')
            d2 = datetime.strptime(last, '%Y-%m-%d')
            days = (d2 - d1).days
            durations.append((slug, ctype, days, count))
        except:
            pass
    
    if durations:
        durations.sort(key=lambda x: x[2], reverse=True)
        summary['timeline_stats'] = {
            'avg_duration_days': sum(d[2] for d in durations) / len(durations),
            'max_duration': {'slug': durations[0][0], 'days': durations[0][2]},
            'min_duration': {'slug': durations[-1][0], 'days': durations[-1][2]},
            'median_duration_days': durations[len(durations)//2][2],
        }
        # Duration by case type
        type_durations = defaultdict(list)
        for slug, ctype, days, count in durations:
            type_durations[ctype].append(days)
        summary['duration_by_type'] = {
            k: {'avg': sum(v)/len(v), 'count': len(v), 'max': max(v), 'min': min(v)}
            for k, v in type_durations.items()
        }

# Cases with negotiations (unique)
neg_cases = set(r[0] for r in patterns['negotiation_rounds'])
summary['cases_with_negotiation'] = len(neg_cases)

# Cases with settlements (unique)
settle_cases = set(r[0] for r in patterns['settlement_mentions'])
summary['cases_with_settlement'] = len(settle_cases)

# Cases with litigation (unique)
lit_cases = set(r[0] for r in patterns['litigation_mentions'])
summary['cases_with_litigation'] = len(lit_cases)

# Cases with decline (unique)
decline_cases = set(r[0] for r in patterns['decline_reasons'])
summary['cases_with_decline'] = len(decline_cases)

# Cases with UIM (unique)
uim_cases = set(r[0] for r in patterns['uim_mentions'])
summary['cases_with_uim'] = len(uim_cases)

# Cases with lien mentions (unique)
lien_cases = set(r[0] for r in patterns['lien_mentions'])
summary['cases_with_liens'] = len(lien_cases)

# Phase change analysis
phase_counts = Counter()
for item in patterns['phase_changes']:
    if len(item) >= 2:
        phase_counts[item[1]] += 1
summary['phase_transitions'] = dict(phase_counts.most_common(20))

# Adjuster pattern breakdown
adj_types = Counter(r[1] for r in patterns['adjuster_patterns'])
summary['adjuster_pattern_types'] = dict(adj_types)

# Unique cases per pattern
summary['unique_cases'] = {
    'negotiation': len(neg_cases),
    'settlement': len(settle_cases),
    'litigation': len(lit_cases),
    'decline': len(decline_cases),
    'uim': len(uim_cases),
    'liens': len(lien_cases),
    'pip': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['pip_mentions'])),
    'unreachable': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['unreachable_client'])),
    'collections': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['collections_anxiety'])),
    'records': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['records_collection'])),
    'demand': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['demand_mentions'])),
    'disbursement': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['disbursement_mentions'])),
    'release': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['release_mentions'])),
    'wc': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['wc_patterns'])),
    'premise': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['premise_patterns'])),
    'sol': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['sol_mentions'])),
    'companion': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['companion_cases'])),
    'funding': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['funding_mentions'])),
    'lost_wages': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['lost_wages'])),
    'minor': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['minor_settlement'])),
    'google_review': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['google_review'])),
    'file_transfer': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['file_transfer'])),
    'intake': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['intake_patterns'])),
    'adjuster': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['adjuster_patterns'])),
    'treatment': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['treatment_mentions'])),
    'esignature': len(set(r[0] for r in patterns['esignature_mentions'])),
}

# Output
with open('/tmp/wiki_patterns.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(summary, f, indent=2, default=str)

print(json.dumps(summary, indent=2, default=str))
